---
title: "Article Title Here"
description: "A one-line summary for listings and social sharing."
author:
  - name:
      given: Diego Filippo
      family: Marino
date: "2026-06-25"
categories:
  - Machine Learning
  - Mathematics
# image: thumbnail.png
# bibliography: references.bib
# jupyter: python3
draft: false
---

## Introduction

Opening paragraph. State the problem, the motivation, and what this article covers. Keep it concise — the reader should know within two sentences whether this is relevant to them.

This article demonstrates every content element available in Quarto. Use it as a starting point: copy this file into a new directory, rename it to `index.qmd`, remove `draft: true`, and replace the content.

## Mathematical content

Inline math uses single dollars: the loss function $\mathcal{L}(\theta)$ measures model error.

Display equations use double dollars:

$$\nabla_\theta \mathcal{L}(\theta) = -\frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} \nabla_\theta \log p_\theta(x_i)$$
 {#eq-gradient}

Reference equations with `@eq-label`: see @eq-gradient above.

A matrix example:

$$A = \begin{bmatrix}
a_{11} & a_{12} \\
a_{21} & a_{22}
\end{bmatrix}, \quad
\lambda \in \sigma(A)$$

## Code

### Static code block (not executed)

``` python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(dim, dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Linear(dim, dim),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return x + self.net(x)
```

### Executable code block

Uncomment `jupyter: python3` in the YAML header to enable execution.

```{{python}}
#| label: fig-example
#| fig-cap: "Example plot generated from code."

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.linspace(-2 * np.pi, 2 * np.pi, 300)
y = np.sin(x) * np.exp(-0.1 * x**2)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
ax.plot(x, y, color="#2a6e6e", linewidth=1.5)
ax.set_xlabel("x")
ax.set_ylabel("f(x)")
ax.set_title("Damped sine wave")
ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Inline code

Use backticks for inline code: the `Adam` optimizer with `lr=3e-4`.

## Text formatting

Regular paragraph with **bold text**, *italic text*, and ***bold italic***. Use `inline code` for function names and variables. Add a [hyperlink](https://quarto.org) or an [internal link](../first-technical-post/index.qmd).

### Unordered list

- First item with some detail
- Second item
  - Nested item
  - Another nested item
- Third item

### Ordered list

1.  First step
2.  Second step
3.  Third step

### Blockquote

> The purpose of computing is insight, not numbers.
>
> — Richard Hamming

## Callouts

::: callout-note
## Note

Use notes for supplementary information that supports the main text.
:::

::: callout-warning
## Warning

Use warnings for common pitfalls or important caveats.
:::

::: callout-tip
## Tip

Use tips for practical recommendations or shortcuts.
:::

::: callout-important
## Important

Use important callouts for critical information the reader must not miss.
:::

## Figures and images

![Placeholder for a figure. Replace with your own image.](https://via.placeholder.com/700x300/f0eeea/65656d?text=Figure+placeholder){#fig-placeholder fig-alt="Placeholder image" width="100%"}

Reference figures with `@fig-label`: see @fig-placeholder.

## Tables

| Method   | Accuracy | Parameters | Training time |
|:---------|:--------:|-----------:|--------------:|
| Baseline |  72.3%   |       1.2M |            2h |
| Proposed |  78.1%   |       1.4M |            3h |
| Ensemble |  80.5%   |       4.2M |            8h |

: Comparison of methods on the benchmark dataset.

Reference tables with `@tbl-label`: see @tbl-results.

## Tabbed content

::: panel-tabset
### PyTorch

``` python
loss = F.cross_entropy(logits, targets)
loss.backward()
optimizer.step()
```

### JAX

``` python
loss, grads = jax.value_and_grad(loss_fn)(params, batch)
updates, opt_state = optimizer.update(grads, opt_state)
params = optax.apply_updates(params, updates)
```
:::

## Footnotes

This claim needs a citation[^1]. This is another footnote[^2].

[^1]: Source or explanation for the first claim.

[^2]: Source or explanation for the second claim.

## Definitions and terminology

Term

:   Definition of the term. Use definition lists for glossary-style entries.

Eigenvalue

:   A scalar $\lambda$ such that $Av = \lambda v$ for some nonzero vector $v$.

## Horizontal rule

Use three dashes for a thematic break:

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Conclusion

Summarize the key findings. State limitations. Suggest directions for future work.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
